mappings.rationale: Production functions, cost analysis, marginal cost, and profit maximization are central to output analysis (A.1, A.2).
The productive process, division of labour, human capital, and the relationship between production and income are foundational to outline A and B.
Labour as a productive input -- workforce deployment, job satisfaction, employment, and the meaning of work -- connects to outline C.1.
Personnel administration, recruitment, employee development, and managing human resources in organizations connect to H.3.
The legal framework for labour -- employment rights, equal pay, and workplace regulation -- is central to C.1.b and C.1.c.
Business administration, production control, motivation, and managerial functions are central to H.4 and C.1.j.
The economic role of entrepreneurs in organizing production and creating business ventures connects to C.1.j.
Business organizations structure production, manage supply chains, and handle outsourcing (H, D).

mappings.vsiAuthor: John O. S. Wilson, John Goddard
mappings.rationale: Commercial and central banking, monetary policy, money markets, and the financial system connect to D.2.
Directly addresses financial accounting, balance sheets, cost accounting, and accounting standards (H.1).
Securities pricing, interest rates, and long-term financial markets connect to D.2.g.
Energy as an input in production -- fossil fuels, nuclear, and renewable sources -- is central to C.3.
Renewable energy technologies as productive inputs -- solar, wind, and batteries -- connect to C.3.
Directly addresses agricultural economics, farming organization, technology's effect on crops, and farm types (E).
Directly addresses advertising methods, market research, and media channels for product promotion (I).
Marketing department functions, competitive advantage, and customer-facing distribution connect to H.5.
International trade arrangements, tariffs, and the regulatory framework for global commerce connect to F.3.
Taxation in the budgetary process and the government's role in production and distribution connect to G.1.
Risk analysis, cost-benefit analysis, and risk assessment are relevant to insurance and risk distribution (J).
Statistical methods -- estimation, sampling, and data analysis -- are used in economic forecasting (D.3).
Technological innovation, intellectual property, and creative destruction transform production processes (A, E.4).
Patents, copyrights, and trademarks serve as institutional arrangements that facilitate production (D).
Regulation of mergers, cartels, and competitive practices in business consolidation connects to H.2.c and G.
International trade, global distribution of resources, and factors influencing cross-border commerce are relevant to outline F.
The historical transformation of production methods, technology, and industrial organization is foundational to outline A and E.4.
Keynesian monetary theory, the quantity theory of money, and government fiscal roles in production connect to D.1 and G.
Smith's foundational theories of trade, production, and the government's role in the economy connect to F.1 and G.
Project management, production scheduling, and risk management in organized business activities connect to H.4.
Capital as a productive input and the business corporation as an institutional arrangement are central to C.4 and H.
Corporate governance, business ethics, and stakeholder accountability are relevant to C.1.j (corporate organization) and the social responsibilities of business enterprises.
